scope: Branch delta past the runtime2 merge (d8c18e257..55da8f529). Production C# net additions: PLang/app/modules/MarkdownTeaching.cs (new, ~140 LOC), PLang/app/modules/this.cs (+63 — MarkdownTeachingRoot, ResolveMarkdownTeachingRoot, WarnOrphansAsync, Describe() integration), PLang/app/goals/goal/steps/step/actions/action/this.cs (+71 — Notes/ModuleNotes/ExamplesMd/ModuleExamplesMd record fields + *Rendered accessors). Plus mechanical [Description]/[Example] attribute deletions across ~130 action-handler files; content migrated to os/system/modules/<module>/{module,<action>}.{description,notes,examples}.md.

build.baseline: tester v2 clean-rebuild baseline stands (C# 2945/2945, plang 204/204 + drift 2/2 across 3 fresh-cache rounds). No production-C# surface changes since tester v2 that would invalidate the build numbers.

attack_surface.exposure: Reads {modulesRoot}/{moduleName}/{actionName}.{notes,examples,description}.md and the module-level {module}.* counterparts via File.ReadAllText. Single call site: app/modules/this.cs:393 inside Describe().
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Build-time host process; loader called only with reflection-derived values.
mitigations: moduleName iterates the compile-time Names[] registry (Namespace of registered handler types)
actionName comes from reflection over handler types — not from program input
Loader is read-only; no writes, no exec
Sink is the LLM compile prompt (excluded per ruleset rule 14) — not a security boundary

attack_surface.exposure: Enumerates modulesRoot subdirectories and reports .md files whose stem is not 'module' and not in the registered action set. Output routed by app.modules.@this.WarnOrphansAsync to actor.Channels.WriteTextAsync(Output, …).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Developer terminal at plang build time.
mitigations: No caller-supplied path component — pure directory walk under modulesRoot
Channel destination is the developer's own terminal — no remote exposure

attack_surface.exposure: Bulk removal of [Description] / [Example] attributes from action handlers; content migrated to repo-tracked .md files.
mitigations: Both sources (attribute strings, .md files) are repo-tracked and developer-authored — equal trust level
Both flow into the same sink (LLM compile prompt) — no new boundary crossed
Spot-check confirmed no behavior/gating/auth/signing/permission code changed on output/*, signing/*, settings/*, mock/action

findings.vector: Actor.Permission.VerifySignature trusts signing.verify integrity without pinning signer identity.
findings.carried_from: filesystem-permission v1/v2 F1 → path-polymorphism v3 F1 — untouched on this branch (file not in C# diff).

findings.vector: permission/this.cs — null-signature row short-circuits to trust.
findings.carried_from: filesystem-permission v1/v2 F2 → path-polymorphism v3 F2 — untouched on this branch.
filesystem-permission v1/v2 F4 → path-polymorphism v3 F4 — untouched on this branch.

observations.note: MarkdownTeachingRoot is a public string? setter on the catalog singleton. Used by tests to stage fixtures in a temp folder. Not reachable from a PLang program (catalog instance not exposed as an action target, no [Code]-attributed parameter type points at it, not IDataWrappable). Host-process trusted code only.

Directory.EnumerateDirectories/EnumerateFiles will follow symlinks on .NET. A planted symlink inside os/system/modules/ could redirect ScanOrphans' walk — but writing there requires repo write access, at which point an attacker would edit C# directly. No new boundary; not scored.
File.ReadAllText has no size guard. Content sink is the LLM compile prompt (rule 14 excluded) and not user-controlled to begin with. DOS/resource-exhaustion concerns are excluded by rules 1/4.
